text:
Market today: Mood is cautious-to-mixed. The real driver is a looming jobs report expected to disappoint, which would normally boost rate-cut hopes — but the Fed independence drama (Trump vs. Cook) adds uncertainty. Rate-sensitive and growth/tech names like yours are in a holding pattern waiting for clarity.

Your holdings: Three need attention. AVGO is your largest position, down nearly 15% — on margin, that's a real dollar hit; watch the $336 stop hard. PLTR is down 11% and flagged SELL — no reason to let this drift further on borrowed money. SPAL is down 12.5% already and it's a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily — every down day costs you twice, and margin on top of leverage is dangerous. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history — size it small and manage manually.

Watch: 1) Friday's jobs number — a bad print could spark a sell-off in your chip/tech names. 2) Any Fed independence news — prolonged uncertainty could pressure growth stocks.

Bottom line: You have two losers (AVGO, PLTR) on margin that need stops enforced now, not later.
